name: alarm_set
description: Schedule a future wake for yourself. Chronos durably stores the alarm and, when it fires, wakes you with `wake_message` as a new turn (optionally resuming `conversation_id`) plus your opaque `payload`. Use kind='once' with either `delay_seconds` (relative, e.g. 600 = in 10 minutes) or `fire_at` (absolute RFC3339, e.g. 2026-06-01T09:00:00Z). Use kind='cron' with `cron_expr` (5-field, @daily/@hourly/etc, or @every 30m) and an optional IANA `timezone` for recurring wakes. Write `wake_message` to your FUTURE self with enough context to act without re-deriving state.

label.description: Short human label for this alarm (for your own listing/recall).

delay_seconds.description: once: fire this many seconds from now. Mutually exclusive with fire_at.

fire_at.description: once: absolute RFC3339 instant to fire at (UTC or with offset). Mutually exclusive with delay_seconds.

timezone.description: cron: IANA timezone for evaluation (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to UTC.

conversation_id.description: Optional conversation to resume into. Omit to start a fresh conversation on wake.

wake_message.description: REQUIRED. The turn delivered to you when the alarm fires — write it as a contextful instruction to your future self.

payload.description: Opaque JSON echoed back to you verbatim on wake (state you want to carry forward).

idempotency_key.description: Optional per-owner dedup key; re-setting the same key returns the existing alarm instead of duplicating.

max_failures.description: Optional wake-delivery retry ceiling before a once alarm is marked failed (default 5).

name: alarm_list
description: List your scheduled alarms (most recent first), including status, next_fire_at, and failure info. Use this to recall what wakes you have pending before setting new ones.

name: alarm_get
description: Fetch one of your alarms by id, including its wake_message, payload, schedule, and current status.

id.description: The alarm id returned by alarm_set / alarm_list.

name: alarm_cancel
description: Cancel one of your active alarms by id so it never fires (recurring cron alarms stop; once alarms are retired). Already-fired or already-cancelled alarms are returned unchanged.
